author: spencer_tb
content: Should this be added to ACDT agenda?

author: chfast
content: I will not be able to make it to the call anyway. But <@968526635735326741> has left some comments in the PR so it can be discussed. The direction seems to be common to work in other EIPs: pay-as-go but don't over-charge-now—refund-later.

author: garyrong
content:
<@425274498732916736> iiuc, you are proposing to 
- perform the checks first: nonce overflow, balance transfer affordability, call depth overflow, 
- access the create destination for: address collision, existence check (the destination will be added to AL)

These steps will be performed first, and then calculate the amount of gas to be forwarded to the create frame.

This change will be very useful in the case the destination is pre-funded, no pre-charge, more gas to be forwarded to create frame, good.

For check ordering, it should be the implementation detail (iiuc), although it's a little bit painful for geth, as we separate the gas calculation and op-execution in two func

author: garyrong
content: but anyway, the eip will impact the pre-funded deployment destination case a lot, but it's the only case the pre-charged state cost shou;ldn't happen

author: chfast
content: Generally good to me. This is what I wanted to propose if not clear from the PR. One detail: put the depth check before the balance check (although it cannot fail in Amsterdam).
